malware.notes: The Gentlemen (Microsoft: Storm-2697) ransomware deployed against IT/enterprise systems (Level 3 MES, logistics and cane-supply scheduling), forcing mills offline. Delivery vector not disclosed.

social.notes: The Gentlemen listed Mackay Sugar on its Tor leak site on 2026-06-15 with a countdown, threatening to publish allegedly stolen data (double-extortion). No ransom figure disclosed; no data published as of 2026-07-02.

name: The Gentlemen (Storm-2697)
external.notes: Ransomware-as-a-service crew active since ~Sept 2025; double-extortion MO; reporting suggests a Russian-speaking founder but actor location is not confirmed, so country left Unknown.

asset.notes: IT/enterprise servers supporting cane supply, logistics scheduling and mill operations (Level 3 MES) were encrypted. Per latest analysis, OT/ICS (PLCs/DCS) were not directly compromised; the mill shutdown was a downstream effect of IT systems going offline.

availability.notes: Two of three Queensland mills (Farleigh and Racecourse) shut down from ~2026-06-10; limited manual crushing resumed at one mill by 2026-06-12 and staged system restoration was ongoing.

confidentiality.notes: Attacker-claimed corporate data theft (double-extortion leak-site listing); the specific nature/extent of stolen data is unconfirmed by the victim and no data had been published as of 2026-07-02. Coded Yes on the basis of confirmed unauthorized access to live systems holding the data plus the attacker's exfiltration claim.

integrity.notes: Ransomware software installed on victim systems; extortion demand constitutes an attempt to alter victim behavior (payment).

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23385
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260702T132320Z

Mackay Sugar Limited (Australia's second-largest raw sugar producer, Queensland) hit by The Gentlemen (Microsoft: Storm-2697) ransomware; incident surfaced 2026-06-10, victim named on the group's Tor leak site 2026-06-15 with a ~10-day countdown to publish allegedly stolen data. As of 2026-07-02 no data has been published and the victim has not confirmed a data breach.

Coding decisions / assumptions:
- timeline.incident set to 2026-06-10 (date the incident surfaced / operations disrupted); actual initial-access date not disclosed. Initial access VECTOR unconfirmed (newest analysis speculates edge-device vuln or compromised credentials; not forensically verified) -> event_chain step 1 coded unk, action.malware.vector = Unknown.
- data_disclosure = Yes based on confirmed unauthorized access to live IT systems holding data plus the double-extortion group's exfiltration claim; the exfiltration itself is ATTACKER-CLAIMED and not victim-confirmed, and no data was leaked as of 2026-07-02. confidentiality.data.variety = Internal (corporate/business data per source 'Corporate' classification); specific data types unknown.
- action.social.variety = Extortion (leak-site threat to publish stolen data); target/vector Unknown; ransom amount not disclosed. plus.attribute.confidentiality.data_abuse = Yes - Data ransomed (leak-site/extortion component).
- actor country Unknown (RaaS crew; reporting suggests Russian-speaking founder but location unconfirmed).
- OT/ICS impact: latest analysis indicates IT-side (Level 3 MES/logistics/scheduling) encryption caused the mill shutdown; no direct PLC/DCS compromise confirmed, so no OT asset coded.
- employee_count '101 to 1000' (~597 permanent staff, ~800 with seasonal workers).
- dbir_year 2027 (incident begin June 2026, Jan-Oct window -> Y+1).

Sources consulted: SecurityWeek; Mackay Sugar official incident notice (mkysugar.com.au); The Record (Recorded Future); Cyber Daily (exclusive); Breachsense; and (newest, independent) Shieldworkz Deep-Dive published 2026-07-02, which corroborates that no data was leaked and the initial-access vector remains unconfirmed — no facts contradicted.

event_chain.summary: Initial access; entry vector not disclosed
Extortion via leak-site listing and countdown

summary: Mackay Sugar Limited, Australia's second-largest raw sugar producer, suffered a ransomware attack by The Gentlemen (Microsoft: Storm-2697) that surfaced on 2026-06-10 and forced two of its three Queensland mills (Farleigh and Racecourse) offline during the crushing season. The group named the company on its Tor-based leak site on 2026-06-15 with a countdown, claiming to have stolen corporate data (double-extortion), though no data had been published and the victim had not confirmed a data breach as of 2026-07-02.
